# Accessibility Checklist: Unified landing page and IA shell

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-002 accessibility requirements for static content, keyboard and screen-reader navigation, semantic route shells, and future enhancement fallback.
**Created**: 2026-06-13
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Static Content And Fallback

- [x] CHK001 Are the critical platform choices and source-vs-generated-payload explanation required to be available as static document content rather than only as visual styling or client-side behavior? [Resolved, Spec FR-020, Contract accessibilityShellContract]
- [x] CHK002 Does the spec define an accessibility fallback rule for later DOC-006 interactive aids so route shells remain usable when JavaScript, custom widgets, or enhanced selectors are unavailable? [Resolved, Spec FR-021, Plan Accessibility Constraints, Contract accessibilityShellContract]
- [x] CHK003 Are route shells that defer full content required to expose audience, useful-now content, deferred owner DOC, evidence, and next steps without hidden JavaScript-only behavior? [Resolved, Spec FR-019, Contract routeShellContentMinimum]

## Keyboard And Focus Requirements

- [x] CHK004 Are keyboard navigation and focus-order requirements specified for the landing-page platform choices, route links, sidebar links, and page-local links? [Resolved, Spec FR-020, Spec FR-021, Plan Phase 1 Design, Data Model Accessibility Shell Contract]
- [x] CHK005 Are requirements clear that DOC-002 should rely on native links and Starlight navigation defaults rather than custom inaccessible widgets for core navigation? [Resolved, Spec FR-009, Spec FR-020, Plan Phase 1 Design, Contract accessibilityShellContract]
- [x] CHK006 Are focus visibility requirements documented for any DOC-002 styling or component choices that could affect native Starlight defaults? [Resolved, Spec FR-021, Plan Accessibility Constraints, Data Model Accessibility Shell Contract]

## Screen Reader And Semantic Structure

- [x] CHK007 Are heading hierarchy and landmark expectations specified for the landing page and route shells so route purpose, owner DOC, success criterion, evidence, and next steps are perceivable by screen readers? [Resolved, Spec FR-020, Spec SC-010, Plan Phase 1 Design, Data Model Accessibility Shell Contract]
- [x] CHK008 Are link purpose requirements specified so Claude Code, Codex, source evidence, and related-route links can be understood out of context? [Resolved, Spec FR-020, Spec FR-021, Data Model Accessibility Shell Contract, Contract accessibilityShellContract]
- [x] CHK009 Are route-shell metadata fields required to appear as user-facing semantic content rather than only as frontmatter or visual labels? [Resolved, Spec FR-007, Spec FR-020, Data Model Top-Level Route Shell]

## Visual And Responsive Accessibility

- [x] CHK010 Are color contrast and non-color-only communication requirements defined for landing-page choices, route status labels, and source-vs-payload callouts if DOC-002 adds custom styles? [Resolved, Spec FR-021, Plan Accessibility Constraints, Data Model Accessibility Shell Contract]
- [x] CHK011 Are touch target and responsive reading-order requirements scoped or intentionally deferred for DOC-002 route shells? [Resolved, Spec FR-021, Spec Assumptions, Data Model Accessibility Shell Contract]
- [x] CHK012 Are accessibility requirements bounded so DOC-002 covers static shell accessibility while DOC-010 still owns broader accessibility testing policy, screenshots, and hardening? [Resolved, Spec Assumptions, Research static accessibility shell decision, Contract accessibilityShellContract]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK013 Can a reviewer objectively check that critical route and platform content is available without browser-side local command execution or JavaScript-only interactions? [Resolved, Spec FR-009, Spec FR-020, Spec SC-003, Spec SC-010]
- [x] CHK014 Can a reviewer objectively check the accessibility impact of later interactive enhancements against a documented static fallback contract? [Resolved, Spec FR-021, Spec SC-010, Contract accessibilityShellContract]
- [x] CHK015 Are the accessibility requirements traceable across spec, plan, data model, route contract, and workflow gate language? [Resolved, Spec FR-020, Spec FR-021, Plan Accessibility Constraints, Data Model Accessibility Shell Contract, Workflow Accessibility result]

## Verification Run 2

- [x] CHK016 Are the static accessibility shell requirements now present in the spec with measurable acceptance criteria? [Resolved, Spec FR-020, Spec FR-021, Spec SC-010]
- [x] CHK017 Are keyboard, focus, heading, link-purpose, color, target, and reading-order requirements represented consistently in plan and data model? [Resolved, Plan Accessibility Constraints, Data Model Accessibility Shell Contract]
- [x] CHK018 Does the route-shell contract now expose the static fallback and future-enhancement boundary for DOC-006 and DOC-010? [Resolved, Contract accessibilityShellContract]
- [x] CHK019 Does the accessibility scope avoid adding DOC-010-owned automated tooling, responsive screenshots, or broad CI policy to DOC-002? [Resolved, Spec Assumptions, Research static accessibility shell decision, Contract accessibilityShellContract.laterOwner]
